I agree that option A, the scapula, does not commonly host significant amounts of brown adipose tissue (BAT). Unlike other locations mentioned which have direct access to major metabolic centers or are exposed areas prone to temperature regulation needs, the scapular area lacks these specific conditions favoring BAT deposition. Additionally, while subcutaneous sites can indeed harbor BAT deposits near important anatomical landmarks, the scapula itself being an osseous structure without extensive vascularization typical of BAT-rich regions further supports this exclusion from containing substantial BAT.
